title: Oral and gut bacteria in stroke patients associated with worse prognosis and mortality
summary: A common bacteria usually found in the mouth and gastrointestinal tract, Streptococcus anginosis, may be abundant in the guts of people with stroke and is associated with a worse prognosis and increased risk of death, according to a preliminary study to be presented at the American Stroke Association's International Stroke Conference 2025.

title: Niger becomes the first country in sub-Saharan Africa to
summary: The Mectizan Donation Program congratulates Niger for achieving the elimination of river blindness, also known as onchocerciasis....

title: Stem cell-derived heart patches show promise for heart failure treatment
summary: Innovative muscle patches derived from stem cells demonstrate efficacy in heart repair, improving function in primates and a human patient, with trials ahead.

title: Urgent warning as cases of 'world's most infectious Victorian disease' surge in UK - 6 signs you must not ignore
summary: CASES of the world's most infectious disease are still on the rise in England - and experts are asking Brits to be aware of the symptoms to help stop

title: Trump administration’s halt of CDC’s weekly scientific report stalls bird flu studies
summary: The Trump administration has intervened in the release of important studies on the bird flu, as an outbreak escalates across the United States.

title: The surprising points of life where you're most vulnerable to ageing - and the very simple steps that can help you stay young
summary: Whereas the previous thinking was that ageing is a linear, gradual process, recent research suggests that it in fact hits you hardest at two distinct points of your life.

title: As drinks targeting kids have soared, there's new guidance on what's healthy
summary: As highly caffeinated energy drinks have become popular with kids, a coalition of health organizations has new guidelines on what's a healthy beverage. Some of the advice may surprise parents.

title: Anxiety reduction with antidepressants confirmed, long-term effects uncertain
summary: A new Cochrane review confirms that antidepressants effectively reduce symptoms of generalized anxiety disorder (GAD) under trial conditions, although there is limited data on long-term usage.

title: Lack of essential vitamins and minerals common in people with type 2 diabetes
summary: A lack of essential vitamins and minerals is common in people with type 2 diabetes, a new study has found.

title: Fresh embryo transfer may improve IVF success for women with low prognosis
summary: Fresh embryo transfer may be a better option than use of frozen embryos for women with a low chance of having a healthy baby by vitro fertilization (IVF), suggests a trial from China published by The BMJ today.
